name: renumbered-dl-squash-merge
query: clean up branches — there are about 20 old DL branches I want to delete if they're already merged
expected_behavior: Runs git cherry main origin/<branch> for each ahead-of-main remote branch
When git cherry emits a '+' line, does NOT immediately classify the branch as unmerged
Greps main's log by the DL number AND by a key phrase from the commit subject to detect squash-merge with renumbering (real cases in this repo: DL-387→DL-343, DL-386→DL-387, DL-388→DL-389)
Only deletes a branch after confirming via cherry OR via main-log subject match
Stops and asks the user when a '+' commit has no semantic match in main
_notes: Edge case — the squash-merge + DL-renumber pattern that motivated this skill. Plain --merged check would miss these and leave branches piling up forever; plain --force delete would orphan genuine WIP. The skill must do both checks.
